theme_notes: Drawn from Iran's renewed Strait of Hormuz blockade and US seizure of an Iranian cargo ship; Champions League quarter-final carnage (Bayern 4-3 Real Madrid, PSG eliminating Liverpool, Barcelona crashing out); Trump's tariff regime hitting $1,500 per US household after the Supreme Court's IEEPA ruling; and Justin Bieber headlining Coachella 2026.
settled: The Strait of Hormuz has more flow than your wallet right now.
Balanced like the Champions League table. And just as fragile.
You're square. Rarer than a functional trade deal in 2026.
youOwe: Less than a Coachella parking spot. Still embarrassing.
The tariff on this debt is higher than the debt itself.
Liverpool's Champions League hopes lasted longer than your budget.
About what Trump's tariffs cost every American per month. You're on brand.
Iran seized a cargo ship for less drama than your receipts.
Bieber grossed more than this per second at Coachella. Sit with that.
Real Madrid lost 4-3 and still looked more composed than your bank account.
Approaching the $1,500 per-household tariff tax. You are the trade war.
Iran blockaded an entire strait. You're blockading your Venmo.
PSG beat Liverpool by 4 goals. You're down to your partner by this much. Same.
This could've been a Coachella ticket. You chose chaos. Classic.
At these tariff rates, your excuses now have import fees.
Bayern hit two late goals to finish Real Madrid. Your debt is equally dramatic.
This exceeds the annual tariff burden per US household. You're a one-person trade war.
If Iran can close the Strait of Hormuz, you can close your tab.
The Strait of Hormuz is shut. Your card should be too.
This debt has its own Champions League bracket. It's reached the semis.
Trump tariffed the entire globe for less economic damage than your lifestyle.
theyOwe: A Bieber ticket stub at Coachella costs more than this. Principles.
A tariff rounding error. That's what they owe you.

Iran would've seized a whole cargo ship over this. Your move.

One tariff-inflated grocery run. Yours. Collect it.
Barcelona crashed out of Europe. Your partner is crashing out of paying you.
The Supreme Court blocked the tariffs. Nothing's stopping you from collecting.
You're running a personal trade deficit. One party. One debt. No resolution.

The Hormuz Strait is closed to tankers. Close your generosity until they pay.

More than the average American's annual tariff hit. They owe you a trade deal.
Iran threatened to choke off global oil supply. Choke off their WiFi first.
This could fund the entire Coachella headliner budget. You're still waiting.
